title: Moczydło, Warsaw

(Polish: [[mɔˈt͡ʂɘ̟.dwɔ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Polish "Help:IPA/Polish")) is a [neighbourhood](/wiki/Districts_and_neighbourhoods_of_Warsaw "Districts and neighbourhoods of Warsaw") in [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw"), [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland"), located within the district of [Ursynów](/wiki/Ursyn%C3%B3w "Ursynów"), in the [City Information System](/wiki/City_Information_System_(Warsaw) "City Information System (Warsaw)") areas of [Kabaty](/wiki/Kabaty "Kabaty") and [Natolin](/wiki/Natolin,_Warsaw "Natolin, Warsaw").[[1]](#cite_note-ew-1)[[2]](#cite_note-msi-2) It a residential area dominated by single-family housing.[[3]](#cite_note-sukp-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Neighbourhood in Warsaw, Poland

This article is about a neighbourhood in Warsaw, Poland. For other uses, see [Moczydło](/wiki/Moczyd%C5%82o_(disambiguation) "Moczydło (disambiguation)").

Neighbourhood in Warsaw, Masovian Voivodeship, Poland

**Moczydło** (Polish: [[mɔˈt͡ʂɘ̟.dwɔ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Polish "Help:IPA/Polish")) is a [neighbourhood](/wiki/Districts_and_neighbourhoods_of_Warsaw "Districts and neighbourhoods of Warsaw") in [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw"), [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland"), located within the district of [Ursynów](/wiki/Ursyn%C3%B3w "Ursynów"), in the [City Information System](/wiki/City_Information_System_(Warsaw) "City Information System (Warsaw)") areas of [Kabaty](/wiki/Kabaty "Kabaty") and [Natolin](/wiki/Natolin,_Warsaw "Natolin, Warsaw").[[1]](#cite_note-ew-1)[[2]](#cite_note-msi-2) It a residential area dominated by single-family housing.[[3]](#cite_note-sukp-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Moczyd%C5%82o,_Warsaw&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b3/Kabaty-Moczyd%C5%82o_ul_Welniania_2.jpg/250px-Kabaty-Moczyd%C5%82o_ul_Welniania_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Kabaty-Moczyd%C5%82o_ul_Welniania_2.jpg)

Historical farming building at Wełniana Street in Moczydło, in 2006.

The earliest known records of Moczydło date to 1528. It was a small farming community, located on the road leading to [Imielin](/wiki/Imielin,_Warsaw "Imielin, Warsaw"), within the [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") [Parish of St. Catherine](/wiki/Church_of_St._Catherine_(Warsaw) "Church of St. Catherine (Warsaw)"). The village was owned and inhabited by a [petty nobility](/wiki/Petty_nobility "Petty nobility"). Between 1580 and 1658, the village and its adjusted farmlands, had an area of around 9 ha, and in 1661, there were 5 houses.[[1]](#cite_note-ew-1)[[4]](#cite_note-malcuzynski-4)[[5]](#cite_note-pawinski-5)

It was owned by the Dąbrowski family until 1725, when it was sold together with [Wolica](/wiki/Wolica,_Warsaw "Wolica, Warsaw") to [Elżbieta Sieniawska](/wiki/El%C5%BCbieta_Sieniawska "Elżbieta Sieniawska"), owner of the [Wilanów Estate](/wiki/Wilan%C3%B3w_Estate "Wilanów Estate"), for the price of 60,000 [złoties](/wiki/Polish_z%C5%82oty "Polish złoty"). She ordered protection of the nearby [Kabaty Woods](/wiki/Kabaty_Woods "Kabaty Woods") from [deforestation](/wiki/Deforestation "Deforestation").[[6]](#cite_note-nowak-6) In 1775, the village had 7 houses, and in 1785, 10 houses. In 1827, it had 10 houses and 80 inhabitants. Between 1850 and 1861, the population of Moczydło fought in court to lower costs of their [feudal duties](/wiki/Feudal_duties "Feudal duties"). Following the [abolition of serfdom](/wiki/Abolition_of_serfdom_in_Poland "Abolition of serfdom in Poland") in 1864, the village was incorporated into the [municipality](/wiki/Gmina "Gmina") of [Wilanów](/wiki/Gmina_Wilan%C3%B3w "Gmina Wilanów"). At the time it was inhabited by 131 people and included 360 ha privately owned farmland, and 36 ha of nobility-owned farmland. In 1905, there were 20 houses and 146 inhabitants.[[1]](#cite_note-ew-1)

In 1879, in Moczydło was a built horse [stable](/wiki/Stable "Stable"), owned by count [Ludwik Józef Krasiński](/w/index.php?title=Ludwik_J%C3%B3zef_Krasi%C5%84ski&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ludwik Józef Krasiński (page does not exist)"), and the village became specialised in breeding horses for the local upper class. In the 1930s, it became a supplier for the newly opened nearby [Służewiec Horse Racing Track](/w/index.php?title=S%C5%82u%C5%BCewiec_Horse_Racing_Track&action=edit&redlink=1 "Służewiec Horse Racing Track (page does not exist)"). It operated until the beginning of the [Second World War](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"). The ruins of the stable survive to the present day, now with the protected status of a [cultural property](/wiki/Cultural_property "Cultural property").[[7]](#cite_note-stable1-7)[[8]](#cite_note-stable2-8)

Following the end of the war, the farmlands in the area were nationalised.[[8]](#cite_note-stable2-8) On 14 May 1951, Moczydło was incorporated into the city of [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw").[[9]](#cite_note-may51-9) In 1956, the area was given by the state to the [Warsaw University of Life Sciences](/wiki/Warsaw_University_of_Life_Sciences "Warsaw University of Life Sciences").[[10]](#cite_note-historiasggw-10)

In 2009, there was opened the [Moczydełko Park](/wiki/Moczyde%C5%82ko_Park "Moczydełko Park").[[11]](#cite_note-park-11)

Currently, the area is a residential neighbourhood, dominated by single-family housing, with some presence of the multifamily housing.[[3]](#cite_note-sukp-3) There remain a few surviving historical structures of the former village.[[7]](#cite_note-stable1-7)[[8]](#cite_note-stable2-8)

## Characteristics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Moczyd%C5%82o,_Warsaw&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Characteristics")]

Moczydło is a residential neighbourhood within the district of [Ursynów](/wiki/Ursyn%C3%B3w "Ursynów") located within the [City Information System](/wiki/City_Information_System_(Warsaw) "City Information System (Warsaw)") areas of [Kabaty](/wiki/Kabaty "Kabaty") and [Natolin](/wiki/Natolin,_Warsaw "Natolin, Warsaw").[[1]](#cite_note-ew-1)[[2]](#cite_note-msi-2) It is dominated by the single-family housing, with some presence of the multifamily housing.[[3]](#cite_note-sukp-3)

Between [Stryjeńskich Street](/wiki/Stryje%C5%84skich_Street,_Warsaw "Stryjeńskich Street, Warsaw") and Wełniana Street is place the [Moczydełko Park](/wiki/Moczyde%C5%82ko_Park "Moczydełko Park"), which is centred on the pond [Moczydło 3](/w/index.php?title=Moczyd%C5%82o_3&action=edit&redlink=1 "Moczydło 3 (page does not exist)").[[11]](#cite_note-park-11) Nearby are also ponds [Moczydło 1](/w/index.php?title=Moczyd%C5%82o_1&action=edit&redlink=1 "Moczydło 1 (page does not exist)") and [Moczydło 2](/w/index.php?title=Moczyd%C5%82o_2&action=edit&redlink=1 "Moczydło 2 (page does not exist)").[[12]](#cite_note-12)
